target.title: fix(ui): preserve visible chat stream text
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (`osolmaz`). Work is in progress until Codex review and CI are green.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Summary

WebChat could show assistant text, then make it disappear.
This change keeps visible streamed text on screen when stale history or a terminal error arrives.
The remaining confirmed part of #67035 is a WebChat state-retention bug, not a local-model latency bug.

## What Changed

The chat controller now treats visible streamed assistant text as something that must be preserved until real history replaces it.
That gives final, aborted, error, and history-reload paths the same retention rule.

- Added shared stream-retention helpers in `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts`.
- Preserved visible `chatStream` when a stale history reload does not contain replacement assistant text.
- Materialized visible streamed text into `chatMessages` before terminal error cleanup.
- Deduped preserved stream text against later final/history assistant messages.
- Strengthened mocked browser coverage for stale history during streaming and terminal error after partial output.

## Linked context

Closes #67035.

Related PR: #77418.

This follows the narrowed maintainer repro from `run_e7fc89b73ec0`, where composer preservation passed but stale refresh and terminal-error stream retention failed.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: visible WebChat assistant stream text disappearing after stale history refreshes or terminal chat errors.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with mocked Control UI browser E2E using Google Chrome.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pnpm lint:core -- ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: controller tests pass 96/96, adjacent gateway/chat-load tests pass 72/72, mocked browser E2E passes 12/12, format/check/lint pass.
- Observed result after fix: streamed text remains visible after stale history resolves and after a terminal chat error.
- What was not tested: true Windows IME input, live LM Studio/local-model streaming, and full production browser behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this PR validates the narrowed confirmed WebChat state bug with mocked Gateway/browser proof. It does not claim to solve every symptom from the original broad issue text.
- Before evidence: Crabbox AWS browser repro `provider=aws`, lease `cbx_126c45468a1a`, run `run_e7fc89b73ec0` failed 2 of 3 checks before this patch.

## Tests and validation

I tested the controller rule directly and the browser path that previously dropped visible stream text.
The new regression coverage covers both stale history and terminal error.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm lint:core -- ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`

Regression coverage added:

- Error events now preserve partial streamed assistant text before clearing stream state.
- Error events do not duplicate streamed text when the server already supplied the same assistant message.
- Stale history reloads keep active visible streamed text.
- Stale history reloads materialize orphaned streamed text when no active run remains.
- Caught-up history clears streamed text when it contains the replacement assistant message.
- Mocked browser E2E verifies visible stream text survives stale startup history and terminal error.

## Risks

The main risk is duplicate assistant messages.
The fix mitigates that by comparing visible stream text against assistant messages after the latest user message and by deduping exact display signatures.

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: No.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: WebChat message reconciliation around stream/history ordering.
- Risk mitigation: focused controller tests plus mocked browser E2E for the two reproduced failure modes.

## Current review state

Next action: run `codex review --base origin/main`, address any P0/P1 findings, then wait for CI.

Still waiting on: Codex review and GitHub CI.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: none yet.

target.title: Replay sessions_yield wait text in WebChat history
target.body:
Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87321

## User-facing bug

WebChat replay could drop a visible `sessions_yield` wait message when that wait text was stored as a transcript tool result instead of a normal assistant text row. In practice, reopening a session could hide text such as "Waiting for child completion.", or expose a raw `toolResult` shape when `chat.message.get` asked for the stored row directly.

## Exact repro

1. Create or load a transcript with an assistant `sessions_yield` tool call.
2. Store the corresponding tool result with `{ "status": "yielded", "message": "Waiting for child completion." }`.
3. Reopen the session through `chat.history` / WebChat replay, or fetch the transcript row through `chat.message.get`.
4. Before this patch, the user-visible wait text could be absent, duplicated in incremental replay, or returned as a raw hidden tool-result row.
5. After this patch, replay projects the yielded wait text as assistant text while preserving the raw `sessions_yield` tool-result row for show-tool-calls/debug/API consumers.

## Fix summary

- Added `sessions_yield` display projection in `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`.
- Tracks pending `sessions_yield` calls by `toolCall`, `tool_use`, and OpenAI `function_call` / `call_id` shapes.
- Mirrors successful yielded tool results into assistant-shaped text marked with `openclawSessionsYieldMirror`.
- Preserves the underlying raw `sessions_yield` tool-result row in `chat.history` instead of consuming it during projection.
- Gives visible `sessions_yield` mirrors a derived transcript id such as `<source-id>:sessions_yield_mirror`, while keeping the original source id addressable for raw `toolResult` reads.
- Keeps hidden `sessions_yield` call rows as raw SSE context so later inline tool results can still be projected correctly.
- Prevents orphan, duplicate, and explicit non-yield tool results from being converted into assistant text.
- Makes `chat.message.get` expand visible `sessions_yield` mirrors from full transcript context while original result ids still return raw tool-result rows.
- Aligns subagent announce summaries with projected yield mirrors.

## Targeted tests

- Added session-history coverage for basic yield projection, separated assistant text, OpenAI `function_call` / `call_id`, missing result call id, unnamed yielded result with one pending call, raw tool-result preservation, orphan results, completed pending context, explicit non-yield tool results, inline SSE dedupe, interleaved tool results, and hidden-call inline context.
- Added gateway `chat.message.get` coverage for derived mirror-id expansion, raw source-result access, duplicate yielded result access, and ordinary tool-result indexing.
- Added subagent announce coverage for projected `sessions_yield` mirror semantics.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WebChat replay now shows transcript-backed `sessions_yield` wait text as normal assistant text without fabricating assistant output from unrelated tool results, while still preserving raw `sessions_yield` tool-result rows for debug/show-tool/API consumers.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/webchat-replay-sessions-yield`, commit `c62f3d1c541f`, rebased onto current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/subagent-yield-output.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts`

Evidence after rebase onto current main:

- Rebased the five-commit branch onto current `origin/main`. The only conflict was in `src/gateway/session-history-state.ts`, where main extracted a shared `buildSnapshot()` helper; resolved by keeping that helper pure and assigning the preserved raw-message tail (`this.rawMessages`) in the constructor and `refreshAsync()` call sites instead.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Changed-file `oxlint` passed with exit code 0.
- Core `tsgo` typecheck (`tsconfig.core.json`) passed with exit code 0 after the conflict resolution.
- Focused Vitest passed after the rebase:
  - `src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts`: 29 tests passed.
  - `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` + `...-b.test.ts`: 62 tests passed.
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts`: 19 tests passed.
- The targeted tests cover:
  - `function_call` / `call_id` projection.
  - explicit `sessions_yield` results that omit result call ids.
  - unnamed yielded results when one `sessions_yield` call is pending.
  - raw yielded `toolResult` rows remaining in projected history.
  - orphan tool results remaining raw.
  - explicit non-yield yielded tool results remaining raw.
  - interleaved other tool results not duplicating wait rows.
  - inline hidden `sessions_yield` call context for following tool results.
  - derived mirror ids such as `msg-yield-result:sessions_yield_mirror` returning the expanded visible assistant mirror.
  - original yielded result ids returning the raw `toolResult`.
  - ordinary tool results staying on the indexed single-message path.
- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues. Its own Vitest attempt was blocked by sandbox EPERM while writing Vite temp files under `node_modules`; the same focused Vitest command passed locally with worktree lock scope and the required permissions.

Observed result after fix: History snapshots include assistant-shaped yield mirrors with wait text and transcript metadata, raw successful `sessions_yield` tool results remain in projected history for debug/show-tool/API consumers, bounded `chat.message.get` expands derived mirror ids using full transcript context, original result ids return raw tool results, and ordinary tool results stay on the existing single-message path.

What was not tested: I did not run the full `pnpm check` / full test matrix locally, and I did not drive a browser WebChat replay manually. Coverage is through focused gateway/session-history/subagent tests plus direct local projection proof.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used local code inspection, targeted regression tests, explicit local command output, and `codex review --base origin/main` before updating the PR.

target.title: Keep core doctor health in contribution order
target.body:
## Summary

This PR integrates structured health checks with the ordered core doctor checks. The goal is to make it cheap and safe to add structured findings, dry-run previews, and diff/effect reporting to existing doctor rules without moving those rules out of the doctor order that already works.

The model is:

- core doctor checks stay owned by `resolveDoctorHealthContributions()` / `createDoctorHealthContribution(...)`, which remains the source of truth for `openclaw doctor` and `openclaw doctor --fix` ordering
- core contributions can now declare `healthChecks` directly; for a single inline check the factory derives `id`, `kind: "core"`, `source: "doctor"`, and `healthCheckIds`
- if a contribution still has legacy `run`, that legacy path remains authoritative for normal `doctor` and real `doctor --fix` mutation
- while legacy `run` is still present, the structured health check should usually add `detect`; any structured `repair` / structured `run` should be preview-only for dry-run, diff, and effect reporting
- the full switchover only happens when legacy `run` is deliberately removed; then the old repair behavior is called from the structured health check and `healthChecks` owns the contribution execution path
- `doctor --lint` runs structured core checks in contribution order first, then appends non-core bundled/plugin registry checks
- the shared health registry is now the extension registry surface: bundled/plugin checks register there and run after the ordered core contribution checks
- core doctor checks do not register into that shared registry for ordering; their order comes from the doctor contribution list
- the broad structured repair bridge is reserved for extension/plugin registry checks rather than becoming a second core repair runner
- the temporary doctor health conversion-plan ledger is removed because contribution ordering and contribution/core-check tests now own the invariant directly

That gives us the migration ladder we wanted: keep existing doctor repair behavior in place while adding structured lint/dry-run/diff data, then move the old repair behavior behind `healthChecks` only when that specific core contribution is ready for full structured ownership. It also keeps the ownership boundary clear: core doctor ordering lives in contributions, while the shared health registry remains the extension point for bundled/plugin checks.


## Concrete Example: #85566

#85566 is the first small rule migration that shows why this PR exists.

The merged bridge shape registered `core/doctor/shell-completion` as structured
health while keeping real repair in the existing positional Doctor path. That
was the safe first step: lint/dry-run data could exist without moving real
`doctor --fix` behavior.

This PR makes the target shape available for follow-up migrations:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:shell-completion",
  label: "Shell completion",
  healthChecks: {
    id: "core/doctor/shell-completion",
    description: "Shell completion profile and cache are ready.",
    async run(ctx, scope) {
      return runShellCompletionHealth(ctx, scope);
    },
  },
  run: runLegacyShellCompletionDoctorRepair,
})
```

That lets `doctor --lint` report `core/doctor/shell-completion` through the
ordered core contribution path, while `openclaw doctor --fix` can keep the
legacy shell-completion repair path in its original Doctor slot until that rule
intentionally moves fully behind `healthChecks`.

Merged bridge example: #85566, merge commit
dc17412c3a9557f16a993a35286d3bb9a4d4e42d.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR changes the doctor health execution boundary: ordered core doctor
contributions supply core lint checks first, while bundled/plugin registry
checks remain the extension surface after core checks.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at
`/root/src/openclaw-86627-core-order`, rebased signed head `c18aa59fc3ac`,
isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, `HOME`, and workspace
under `/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node`.

Proof 1: real command-path ordering with a registered non-core health check.
The proof enabled the bundled Policy plugin, then used the same sequence as
`doctor --lint`: read config, register bundled health checks, resolve ordered
core contribution checks, then append non-core registry checks.

Output:

```json
{"orderedSubset":["core/doctor/gateway-config","core/doctor/command-owner","policy/policy-jsonc-missing"],"coreCount":19,"extensionCount":52}
```

This shows the registered non-core Policy check is appended after the ordered
core Doctor checks.

Proof 2: real source-entry `doctor --lint --json` with selected core and
registered non-core checks.

Command shape:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/state/openclaw.json \
HOME=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/home \
CI=1 TERM=dumb \
node --import tsx src/entry.ts doctor --lint --json \
  --only core/doctor/gateway-config \
  --only core/doctor/command-owner \
  --only policy/policy-jsonc-missing
```

Output:

```json
{"ok":false,"checksRun":3,"checksSkipped":68,"findings":[{"checkId":"core/doctor/gateway-config","severity":"warning","message":"gateway.mode is unset; gateway start will be blocked.","path":"gateway.mode","fixHint":"Run `openclaw configure` and set Gateway mode (local/remote), or `openclaw config set gateway.mode local`."},{"checkId":"policy/policy-jsonc-missing","severity":"warning","message":"policy.jsonc is missing for the enabled Policy plugin.","source":"policy","path":"policy.jsonc","fixHint":"Restore policy.jsonc or add the policy artifact for this workspace."},{"checkId":"core/doctor/command-owner","severity":"info","message":"No command owner is configured. Owner-only commands (/diagnostics, /export-trajectory, /config, exec approvals) have no allowed sender.","path":"commands.ownerAllowFrom","fixHint":"Set commands.ownerAllowFrom to your channel user id, e.g. `openclaw config set commands.ownerAllowFrom '[\"telegram:123456789\"]'`."}]}
```

Exit code: `1`, expected because the selected checks intentionally report
findings.

What was not tested:
No plugin package was installed. The non-core registry proof uses the bundled
Policy plugin as the registered extension-health surface.

## How A Core Rule Upgrades

Before an upgrade, a rule is just an ordered doctor contribution:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
})
```

For the safe in-place upgrade, the rule keeps its old `run` and adds one inline health check beside it. Legacy `run` still owns real doctor behavior; `healthChecks.detect` supplies structured lint findings:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async detect(ctx) {
      return detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx);
    },
  },
})
```

If the rule can provide dry-run/diff/effect detail before the full switchover, it can add structured repair as a preview-only path while legacy `run` still owns real mutation:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async detect(ctx) {
      return detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx);
    },
    async repair(ctx, findings) {
      return previewSomeRuleRepair(ctx, findings);
    },
  },
})
```

When the rule is ready to be fully structured, remove legacy `run` and call the old repair behavior from the structured check. At that point `healthChecks` owns the contribution execution path:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async run(ctx, scope) {
      const findings = await detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx, scope);
      if (!ctx.repair && !ctx.previewRepair) {
        return { findings };
      }
      if (ctx.previewRepair) {
        return previewSomeRuleRepair(ctx, findings);
      }
      return runSomeRuleRepairAsStructured(ctx, findings);
    },
  },
})
```

When one fully structured contribution owns multiple structured checks, each check names its own ID explicitly:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:browser",
  label: "Browser",
  healthChecks: [
    {
      id: "core/doctor/browser",
      description: "Browser readiness is valid.",
      async detect(ctx) {
        return collectBrowserReadinessFindings(ctx);
      },
    },
    {
      id: "core/doctor/browser-clawd-profile-residue",
      description: "Legacy browser profile residue is absent.",
      async detect(ctx) {
        return detectBrowserResidueFindings(ctx);
      },
      async repair(ctx, findings) {
        return previewOrArchiveBrowserResidue(ctx, findings);
      },
    },
  ],
})
```

If a check has repair behavior, it is expected to honor the existing dry-run/diff/effects conventions.

## Validation

- Rebased onto `origin/main` at `b9933b2ec11`; resolved doctor conflicts by
  preserving this PR's contribution-owned core ordering and current main's
  Gateway SecretRef `--allow-exec` guards, `allowExecSecretRefs`, auth
  profile `api_key` alias repair, skipped Gateway health forwarding,
  runtime tool schema, heartbeat template, and read-only model-catalog
  behavior.
- `git diff --check`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts docs/cli/doctor.md`
- `node node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-auth-token.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000` (5 files, 109 tests)
- Added focused regression coverage that the contribution-owned paths preserve Gateway auth `--allow-exec` SecretRef resolution, forward `allowExecSecretRefs` to Gateway service repair, skip Gateway health probes for exec SecretRefs unless `--allow-exec` is set, keep auth-profile `api_key` alias repair wired, and forward skipped Gateway health to daemon repair.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- Rebased signed head: `ea8e3d0f334`.

## Updated In-Place Upgrade Stack

These are the doctor PRs in the in-place upgrade series. Each one keeps the existing contribution order and preserves legacy real repair unless that PR explicitly removes the legacy run path.

| PR | Area | Shape |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #85566 | Shell completion | Structured lint health is owned by the existing doctor:shell-completion contribution. |
| #84290 | UI freshness | Structured lint/dry-run effects report missing or stale Control UI assets, while real build/rebuild stays on the existing doctor UI freshness path. |
| #84366 | Session locks | Structured lint/dry-run effects report stale lock cleanup, while real cleanup stays on the existing session-lock doctor path. |
| #84450 | Config audit scrub | Next small config/state slice: structured lint/dry-run effects report historical config-audit argv redaction cleanup, while real scrub stays on the existing config-audit doctor path. |
| #84326 | Sandbox registry | Structured lint/dry-run effects report legacy sandbox registry migrate/remove/quarantine work, while real migration stays on the existing sandbox doctor path. |
| #84340 | Gateway extra services | Structured lint/dry-run effects report extra gateway-like services and legacy cleanup previews, while real cleanup stays on the existing gateway-services doctor path. |

The remaining older doctor repair drafts should be rebased onto this PR and reduced to this same in-place shape before they are presented as part of the series.

target.title: fix(agents): skip auth gate for CLI-owned transport
target.body:
### Summary
- Skip the model-fallback auth cooldown gate for explicitly configured CLI agent runtimes such as `anthropic/*` with `agentRuntime.id: "claude-cli"`.
- Keep the attempt-level auth-profile selection guard so CLI-owned transports preserve only `authProfileProvider` when auth-profile forwarding is disabled.

### What changed after review
- Rebased onto current `main`.
- Added the fallback-boundary fix requested by review: CLI runtime candidates now set `skipsProviderAuthCooldown: true` before the fallback loop evaluates stale OpenClaw provider auth state.
- Added production-path coverage through `runWithModelFallback` with `anthropic/*`, `claude-cli`, and stale provider auth state.
- Kept the direct `runAgentAttempt` coverage showing `authProfileId` stays unset while the CLI-owned route still runs.

### Real behavior proof
After-fix evidence from the live OpenClaw PR branch at `78ddcb5bdc61030285dc9baed100e74c184aa949`:

```text
$ gh pr checks 88551 -R openclaw/openclaw
Real behavior proof  pass  11s
check-prod-types     pass  55s
check-test-types     pass  1m20s
checks-node-agentic-agents-core-auth     pass  57s
checks-node-agentic-agents-core-runtime  pass  1m22s
checks-node-agentic-cli                  pass  1m9s
checks-node-auto-reply-reply-agent-runner pass 1m3s
```

The after-fix branch includes a production-path regression for the CLI-owned transport shape: `runWithModelFallback` enters the run callback for `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` with `agentRuntime.id: "claude-cli"` even when `anthropic:default` is disabled in OpenClaw auth state. The direct attempt coverage also shows the `codex-cli` route still runs with `authProfileId` unset when auth-profile forwarding is disabled.

### Verification
- PR CI covers the touched model-fallback and CLI attempt execution suites.
- The diff exercises both layers called out in review: the fallback pre-attempt auth gate and the downstream attempt auth-profile selection.

target.title: fix(agents): classify read-only shell commands as non-mutating
target.body:
## Summary

- classify only simple, clearly read-only `exec`/`bash` shell commands as non-mutating
- keep ambiguous shell commands fail-closed as mutating, including heredocs, redirection, pipes, app starts, and mutating `git`/`gh` subcommands
- cover the regression with focused classifier tests for `sed -n`, `rg`, `git status/diff`, and read-only `gh` queries

## Context

This restores the intent of `6b05916c14` (`fix: gate Telegram exec tool warnings behind verbose mode`) without reverting `658a30b42f` (`fix(agents): surface exec failures after claimed success`).

`exec` and `bash` currently fall through as always-mutating, so read-only investigation failures like `sed -n ...` or `gh search prs ...` can surface as final tool warnings even when the assistant already produced a useful response. This patch keeps the claimed-success protection for mutating or ambiguous commands while letting obviously read-only probes use the existing non-mutating warning policy.

The allowlist is intentionally narrow and subcommand-aware. Examples that remain mutating:

- `python3 <<'PY' ...`
- `npm start`
- `cat file > out`
- `rg foo | wc -l`
- `sed -i ...`
- `git checkout ...`
- `git branch -D ...`
- `git diff --output=...`
- `gh pr create ...`
- `gh issue comment ...`
- `gh api --method POST ...`

## Test plan

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/tool-mutation.ts src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/tool-mutation.ts src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx -e "<classifier smoke test>"`

I also attempted:

- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts`

Both Vitest runs stalled locally during the Rolldown build phase with repeated `PLUGIN_TIMINGS` warnings before reaching test execution, so I stopped them and used the targeted formatter/linter/smoke checks above.

target.title: fix(openai): harden Codex OAuth callback cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #81865.

This updates the OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth flow to cover the failures reproduced locally:

- token exchange and refresh use a host-scoped fake-IP SSRF policy for `auth.openai.com`, so proxy stacks that resolve public OpenAI hosts into fake-IP ranges do not block the token request
- malformed/non-JSON successful token responses now return structured OAuth failures without including response-body fragments or parser details
- browser-success OAuth now aborts any local manual-entry prompt that was started by the fallback timer, so the CLI can exit cleanly after the callback/token path wins
- remote/VPS manual prompts are deferred until the flow asks for manual input, so the prompt can receive the cancellation signal

This PR was prepared with AI assistance.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth.runtime.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-oauth-flow.test.ts src/wizard/clack-prompter.test.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-chatgpt-oauth.test.ts`
  - Passed 3 Vitest shards: plugin OAuth helper/provider tests, wizard prompt tests, and OpenAI OAuth runtime/flow tests.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
  - Passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
  - Passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - Passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - Passed.
- `git diff --check`
  - Passed.
- Local Codex review
  - No actionable correctness findings after the follow-up fix.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth login should complete in a real browser-backed flow when the callback wins, persist an OpenAI OAuth auth profile, set the requested default model, and exit without leaving the manual prompt/callback server running. Token exchange should also tolerate fake-IP proxy DNS for the public OpenAI token host without broad private-network allowance.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source worktree on macOS with an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; OpenAI OAuth was completed through the user's browser session.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created an empty isolated config, ran `node scripts/run-node.mjs config validate`, then ran `node scripts/run-node.mjs models auth login --provider openai --method oauth --set-default`. After the command exited, a small local check read only the isolated config/auth-profile file count, then the temp root was removed and `lsof -nP -iTCP:1455 -sTCP:LISTEN` plus a process search checked for leftovers.

Evidence after fix: The isolated login command exited `0`; the post-login check reported `defaultModel: "openai/gpt-5.5"` and `authProfileFileCount: 1`; the cleanup trap printed `CLEANED_PROOF_ROOT`; the follow-up listener/process checks found no `localhost:1455` listener and no remaining `models auth login --provider openai` process.

Observed result after fix: The browser callback/token path completed successfully, the isolated OpenAI OAuth auth profile was written, the isolated config default model was set to `openai/gpt-5.5`, the CLI returned to the shell without a stuck manual-entry prompt, and the temporary auth/config state was removed.

What was not tested: Full repository suites were not run. No proof artifacts, screenshots, videos, or credentials were committed.

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` → `cron show` → `cron run` (manual, triggers error) → `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) → `cron list` (job present) → `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed ✅ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` → `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` → `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged ✅ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged ✅ |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, or failure-alert cooldown.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `3d32c9baff`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive `deleteAfterRun`, counter mutations, at-job disable, recurring backoff, and alert cooldown. The 12 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (includes all 12 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **55 tests passed** (284ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (55 tests) 284ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **10 tests passed** (63ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 63ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 13ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1000 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1000 passed (1002)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 12 new regression tests pass, confirming that manual runs preserve one-shot jobs (`deleteAfterRun`), do not increment/reset counters, do not disable at-jobs on error, do not rewrite recurring `nextRunAtMs` with backoff, and do not clear failure-alert cooldown. Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all 5 state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting-only changes via oxfmt). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, 2026-05-30)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, failure-alert cooldown, or skipped-run alert counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive all 5 scheduled-state effects plus skipped-run alert paths. The 14 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (all 14 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **56 tests passed** (234ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 234ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **11 tests passed** (92ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (11 tests) 92ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 10ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1002 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1002 passed (1004)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. Pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 14 new regression tests pass, covering all 6 guarded state effects including the newly-added manual skipped-run isolation:
  - Manual `deleteAfterRun` at-job → NOT deleted ✅
  - Manual error → counters NOT incremented ✅
  - Manual success → counters NOT reset, cooldown NOT cleared ✅
  - Manual at-job error → NOT disabled, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual every-job error → backoff NOT applied, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped at-job → consecutiveSkipped NOT incremented, alert NOT fired, all state preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped run with `includeSkipped: true` → failure alert NOT emitted ✅

  Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting + test coverage changes). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. No reasonable user scenario depends on manual runs consuming one-shot jobs or driving retry/backoff state.

## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`, 2026-06-02)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `lastRunWasManual` field was missing from the `CronJobStateSchema` and `CronJobStatePatchSchema` in `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/cron.ts`. Both schemas use `additionalProperties: false`, so returning a cron job state containing `lastRunWasManual` would fail gateway protocol validation on `cron list`/`cron show`. Added `lastRunWasManual: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean())` to both schemas.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`), Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `ccb89e3f11`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On the previous commit (`45efd1abdd`), `CronJobStateSchema` did not include `lastRunWasManual`. Any cron job state object with `lastRunWasManual: true` (set by `timer.ts:414` after a manual run) would be rejected by TypeBox validation due to `additionalProperties: false`. The `CronJobStatePatchSchema` was similarly missing the field, preventing state patches that include it.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" \
  -v /app/node_modules \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" \
  node:24-bookworm \
  bash -c '
    cd /app && npm install 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
      src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts 2>&1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 335ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 54ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 31ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts (3 tests) 3ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts (2 tests) 0ms

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  86 passed (86)
   Start at  07:37:28
   Duration  3.44s (transform 2.31s, setup 280ms, import 2.62s, tests 424ms, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 86 tests pass including:
  - `timer.regression.test.ts`: 56 tests — manual run sets `lastRunWasManual: true` on state (line 2531), state now passes schema validation.
  - `service.restart-catchup.test.ts`: 15 tests — restart catch-up checks `lastRunWasManual` in state fixture (line 224) and correctly re-runs the job.
  - `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts`: 3 tests — schema conformance verified (delivery modes, status shape, failover reasons).
  - `cron-protocol-schema.test.ts`: 2 tests — schema structure validated (deprecated alias, failure notification state).

- **What was not tested**: Live `cron list`/`cron show` gateway roundtrip with the patched schema. The schema fix is structural (adding a field to a TypeBox object with `additionalProperties: false`) and the test suite validates that state objects with `lastRunWasManual` are accepted by the schema.

## Live Gateway CLI roundtrip proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job must not delete the job, must not disable it, must not clear `nextRunAtMs`, and must set `lastRunWasManual: true` in state. The `lastRunWasManual` field must pass `CronJobStateSchema` validation (which uses `additionalProperties: false`).
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64, OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, OpenClaw Gateway built from source at commit `ccb89e3f11`.
- **Steps**:
  1. Built OpenClaw from source on the PR branch
  2. Started Gateway inside Docker with `gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth token`
  3. Created a `deleteAfterRun` at-job via `cron add`
  4. Manually ran the job via `cron run` (resulted in error due to missing codex-linux-arm64 in Docker — this is the interesting case: manual error on a deleteAfterRun job)
  5. Verified job survival, `lastRunWasManual: true`, `enabled: true`, and `nextRunAtMs` preserved via `cron show` and `cron list`
  6. Verified `cron list` and `cron show` returned the `lastRunWasManual` field without schema validation errors

- **Observed result**: After manual `cron run` (which triggered an error due to missing codex dependency in Docker):
  - Job **survived** — still present in `cron list` and `cron show` ✅
  - `enabled: true` — manual error did NOT disable the at-job ✅
  - `nextRunAtMs: 1798732799000` — preserved, NOT cleared ✅
  - `deleteAfterRun: true` — still set, NOT consumed by manual run ✅
  - `lastRunWasManual: true` — new field present and accepted by schema ✅
  - No `consecutiveErrors` field — manual error did NOT increment counter ✅
  - Run history shows `runId: "manual:..."` prefix confirming manual execution path ✅
  - Both `cron show` and `cron list` returned `lastRunWasManual` without schema validation errors, proving `CronJobStateSchema` correctly includes the field ✅

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` (would require a working LLM provider in Docker). The `shouldDelete` guard path (`wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual`) is exercised by unit tests. Multi-node cluster, production load.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `8596607fca`, 2026-06-02 — backoff fix)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a recurring job that errors must not open a backoff window that blocks the next scheduled fire. Previously, `isErrorBackoffPending()`, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()`, `isPendingErrorBackoffSlot()`, and `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` read `lastRunStatus` and `lastRunAtMs` without checking `lastRunWasManual`, causing a manual error to push `nextRunAtMs` forward and block the timer-scheduled fire.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `8596607fca`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` does not check `lastRunWasManual`. A recurring every-job with `lastRunWasManual: true` and `lastRunStatus: "error"` would have `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` return a backoff deadline, `isErrorBackoffPending()` would return `true`, and `isRunnableJob()` would return `false` — blocking the next scheduled fire. `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` would also push `nextRunAtMs` forward to the backoff floor. Test file authored as part of this PR.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Unit tests in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c \
  "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts"

# Live Gateway CLI in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  -e OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-test \
  -e OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=test-token-123 \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c '
    mkdir -p /tmp/openclaw-test
    echo "{\"gateway\":{\"port\":19001,\"bind\":\"loopback\"}}" > /tmp/openclaw-test/openclaw.json
    node openclaw.mjs gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --token test-token-123 --port 19001 &
    sleep 8
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-at-delete --at "2026-06-02T23:00:00Z" --message reminder --delete-after-run --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>, cron list
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-recurring --every 10s --message "recurring check" --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>
    kill %1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

<details>
<summary>Docker unit test output (82 tests pass)</summary>

```

 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 44ms

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.85s

<details>

```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. **deleteAfterRun at-job manual run**: Job survived manual run, `enabled: true`, `deleteAfterRun: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅
  2. **Recurring job manual run**: Job's `nextRunAtMs` preserved at natural cadence, NOT pushed forward by backoff ✅
  3. **Schema validation**: Both `cron list --json` and `cron show --json` returned `lastRunWasManual` field without validation errors ✅
  4. **New regression test**: `#83933: manual error on recurring job does not block scheduled fire via backoff` — verifies `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` returns `undefined` for manual errors, `isRunnableJob()` returns `true` within theoretical backoff window, `recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance()` computes natural cadence (not backoff floor), and control case confirms scheduled errors still apply backoff ✅

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` on recurring job (requires working LLM in Docker). Multi-node cluster, production load, actual delivery to chat channels.

target.title: fix #87768: [Bug]: push to talk mac os companion app hard codes thinking low
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: macOS voice sends now omit inherited thinking, but the gateway contract also needs to keep explicit thinking overrides when a caller intentionally sets one.
- Solution: Add a macOS gateway regression test proving `chat.send` still serializes an explicit `thinking` override.
- What changed: `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/GatewayConnectionControlTests.swift` adds the explicit non-nil `thinking` case next to the inherited-default omission test.
- What did NOT change: no production code, no voice routing, no provider/model policy, no gateway protocol schema.

Fixes #87768

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** macOS gateway `chat.send` payloads omit `thinking` when inheriting defaults, while preserving an explicit caller-provided override.
- **Real environment tested:** GitHub Actions macOS 26.3, Apple Swift 6.2.3, OpenClaw worktree SHA 152361e.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  swift --version
  swift test --package-path apps/macos --parallel --enable-code-coverage --show-codecov-path
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** macos-swift check passed for this PR head. Relevant terminal output from the macOS runner:
  ```text
  ProductName:        macOS
  ProductVersion:     26.3
  Apple Swift version 6.2.3 (swiftlang-6.2.3.3.21 clang-1700.6.3.2)
  if swift test --package-path apps/macos --parallel --enable-code-coverage --show-codecov-path; then
  /Users/runner/_work/openclaw/openclaw/apps/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/codecov/OpenClaw.json
  macos-swift: SUCCESS
  Real behavior proof: SUCCESS
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The macOS Swift test suite completed successfully, including the gateway payload tests that assert inherited thinking is omitted and explicit `thinking: "low"` is included when intentionally provided.
- **What was not tested:** live macOS companion app plus Ollama push-to-talk was not rerun; the macOS CI test exercised the changed gateway payload contract directly.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Swift package test
- Target test file: `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/GatewayConnectionControlTests.swift`
- Scenario locked in: `chat.send` includes an explicit non-nil `thinking` override while the adjacent inherited-default test keeps omitted `thinking` pinned.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the gateway request serialization contract directly without changing runtime voice behavior already fixed on main.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: the original macOS voice path forced `thinking: "low"`; after the main fix removed that forced override, the missing guardrail was the opposite side of the contract: explicit overrides must still survive serialization.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no macOS gateway test proving explicit `thinking` remains present when a caller intentionally sets it.

target.title: Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolate QMD mcporter daemon/config state per agent by writing an agent-scoped mcporter config and passing it to mcporter calls.
- Preserve the configured mcporter server definition instead of replacing custom/remote server config with a hard-coded local `qmd mcp` entry.
- Keep QMD model/env settings flowing only through the generated stdio server entry, while stripping QMD-scoped XDG env from mcporter's own process environment.
- Tolerate mcporter daemon log lines before JSON responses so warm daemon calls do not break memory search parsing.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** QMD memory search through mcporter must keep each agent on its own mcporter config/daemon state while preserving the configured `qmd` mcporter server and avoiding QMD-scoped XDG env leakage into mcporter's own process env.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node `v24.15.0`, `mcporter 0.10.2`, `qmd 2.1.0`, configured agents `hex` and `trump`, and the configured mcporter `qmd` stdio server. Home paths are redacted as `~`; private snippets are omitted.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
node --version
mcporter --version
qmd --version
mcporter config get qmd --json
pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent hex --json
pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent trump --json
pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent hex --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent trump --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
node -e '<read generated ~/.openclaw/agents/{hex,trump}/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json and print redacted command/env summary>'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output, redacted:

```text
node --version -> v24.15.0
mcporter --version -> 0.10.2
qmd --version -> qmd 2.1.0

mcporter config get qmd --json
{
  "name": "qmd",
  "source": { "kind": "local", "path": "~/.mcporter/mcporter.json" },
  "transport": "stdio",
  "command": "~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd",
  "args": ["mcp"]
}

pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent hex --json
  agentId=hex backend=qmd files=1310 chunks=1310 dirty=false vector.available=true vector.semanticAvailable=true embeddingProbe.ok=true collections=4

pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent trump --json
  agentId=trump backend=qmd files=204 chunks=204 dirty=false vector.available=true vector.semanticAvailable=true embeddingProbe.ok=true collections=4

pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent hex --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
  exited 0; results.length=1; first.source=memory; first.score=0.93

pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent trump --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
  exited 0; results.length=1; first.source=sessions; first.score=0.93

Generated mcporter config summaries:
hex:   path=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json   imports=[] command=~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd args=["mcp"] XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-config QMD_CONFIG_DIR=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-config/qmd XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-cache
trump: path=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json imports=[] command=~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd args=["mcp"] XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-config QMD_CONFIG_DIR=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-config/qmd XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-cache
```

**Observed result after fix:** Both agents reported healthy QMD vector readiness (`embeddingProbe.ok=true`, `vector.available=true`, `vector.semanticAvailable=true`), both `memory search` commands returned one result successfully, and the generated mcporter configs were separate per agent while preserving the configured `qmd` stdio command.

**What was not tested:** I did not test a live remote HTTP mcporter server; that preservation path is covered by the focused unit test added in this PR.
